chosen_action.title: Keep same-fingerprint churn guards alive across a new live-action review window and pause duplicate guard churn

why_this_action.summary: The selector was still returning measurement_hold at 2026-05-25 09:24 and 09:31 Europe/Berlin even though the execution board still had no truthful do-now packet and the same execution-board fingerprint already had a third-strike churn guard plus prior guard follow-through logs. A fresh publisher email extended the short review window and accidentally made the selector forget the existing same-fingerprint guard state, which reopened fake-progress hold behavior.
shared_findings_used: agents/marketing/logs/distribution_lane_latest.json: latest lane had fallen back to measurement_hold with no short-window release recorded
drafts/marketing_execution_board_latest.md: execution board still says no truthful do-now packet exists in the current review window
agents/marketing/logs/marketing_2026-05-25_072249_distribution_architecture_guard_follow_through.json: this fingerprint was already acknowledged under the churn guard
agents/marketing/logs/marketing_2026-05-25_nxcode_publisher_outreach.json: a real publisher send moved the short review window without changing the empty-board truth

agents/marketing/logs/marketing_2026-05-25_093032_recent_live_external_dedupe_repair.json: recent live external counting was already repaired, so the remaining bad state was guard-state forgetting rather than count inflation

result.summary: Patched distribution-lane guard-state accounting so same-fingerprint churn guards survive a newer live-action review window, added selector regression coverage for the active-window pause case, then re-ran the 2026-05-25 09:31 lane selection/execution so the latest lane became distribution_architecture_guard_pause instead of measurement_hold.

replacement_condition: If the board fingerprint changes or a genuinely new executable packet appears and the selector still pauses under the old guard, escalate into explicit blocker-state diffing rather than another hold or duplicate guard note.
